snapshot.scope: Independent review of the personal-Agent architecture reset: Pi semantic ownership, submitted-turn authority, closed high-risk effects, Provider Send, deletion inventory, risk-tiered verification, budgets, macOS-first sequencing, and unchanged product status.

reviewers.task: Personal Agent architecture reset contract review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning reviews only contracts and executable governance; Development still owns every implementation deletion, rewrite, security test, package and runtime artifact.

dimensions.summary: The start contract routes ordinary work to one semantic owner, Pi, while Host capability, authority, persistence and recovery owners stay explicit.
Security, privacy, runtime, Job, Provider and UI owners share submitted-turn authority, a closed high-risk list and no ordinary approval state machine.
Affected Requirements and Exits are planned, AR1 through AR4 are explicit, and the reset cannot promote product status.
The machine inventory gives Development exact DELETE, REWRITE and KEEP targets, no-growth guards, owner budgets and staged verification nodes.
Decisions are defined once in canonical owners; ordinary PRs avoid full snapshot ceremony while architecture, merge-candidate and main retain complete review.

verification.evidence: Executable guards freeze AR1-AR4, legacy-mechanism no-growth, implementation dispositions, owner budgets and planned status.
Canonical Job reliability plus submitted-turn, high-risk and Provider-send contracts remain single-owned and fail safe.
The full merge-candidate governance suite passes after trace, semantic and reviewed-snapshot regeneration.

coordination.compatibility: Legacy internal approval and Host-orchestration mechanisms are intentionally not a compatibility target; durable user knowledge, sources, credentials and recoverable data remain protected.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning freezes the contract and machine inventory; Development executes AR1-AR4 before ordinary feature work resumes, with no Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

nonBlockingRisks: Current product code still contains the bounded legacy permission and model-egress mechanisms until AR1 lands.
Current Host ingest/retrieval orchestration remains bounded deletion debt until AR2 lands.
Windows and Linux qualification remains deferred and unclaimed during early macOS-first development.
